main_ingredients: thick broth (chicken or vegetable stock base)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Lion's Mane Mushroom Thick Broth Yee Mee is approximately 400 g, comprising a bowl of pre-fried egg noodles submerged in a thick, starchy mushroom broth with Lion's Mane mushroom pieces.

nutrient_source.title: Noodles, egg, cooked; Mushroom, cooked; Broth, chicken, ready-to-serve — composite estimation from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Lion's Mane Mushroom Thick Broth Yee Mee exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by component analysis: Yee Mee noodles (deep-fried egg noodles, ~55% of dish weight) referenced against USDA SR Legacy entry for egg noodles cooked (FDC ID 20312) and deep-fried noodle analogue; Lion's Mane mushroom (~15% of dish weight) referenced against USDA FoodData Central entry for Hericium erinaceus / mushroom cooked; thick broth (~30% of dish weight) referenced against USDA chicken broth ready-to-serve. Weighted composite per 100 g calculated. FAO/INFOODS SEA tables consulted for regional noodle dish cross-check.

gi_source.title: Noodles, instant, cooked — GI analogous estimate; Egg noodles cooked GI reference from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Lion's Mane Mushroom Thick Broth Yee Mee. Yee Mee are deep-fried egg noodles that are rehydrated in broth; GI estimated at 52 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked egg noodles (GI ~46–54, mean ~52) and instant noodles cooked (GI ~47–58). The thick starchy broth and mushroom content are not expected to substantially raise GI above the noodle base. GL per serving = (52 × 66 g carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = 34.3, rounded to 34.5. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55 = low).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from broth and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single 400 g restaurant serving delivers approximately 1,680 mg sodium, representing 73–84% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000–2,300 mg; request reduced-sodium broth or consume a smaller portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined egg noodles in thick starchy broth
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that a standard 400 g serving carries a glycemic load of approximately 34–35, which is high; portion control to half a serving and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate fat from deep-fried noodle base
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should note the high sodium load (~1,680 mg per serving) and moderate fat content from the deep-fried Yee Mee noodles; Lion's Mane mushroom provides beta-glucan fibre with potential cardioprotective properties, but the overall sodium burden warrants portion restriction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lion's Mane Mushroom Thick Broth Yee Mee is a Chinese-Singaporean noodle dish featuring Yee Mee (伊面, Yi Mian), a type of pre-fried egg noodle with a distinctive golden colour and slightly chewy, sponge-like texture that readily absorbs broth. The dish is prepared by simmering the noodles in a thick, starchy broth made from chicken or vegetable stock thickened with cornstarch, seasoned with soy sauce, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil, and topped with Lion's Mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus, 猴头菇), a prized culinary and medicinal mushroom with a mild, seafood-like flavour and meaty texture. Lion's Mane mushroom is valued in traditional Chinese medicine and modern nutritional science for its beta-glucan content, potential neuroprotective properties, and immune-modulating effects. The dish is served hot in a bowl and is commonly found in Chinese restaurants and food courts across Singapore. Nutritionally, a 400 g restaurant serving provides approximately 472 kcal, 19 g protein, 16 g fat, and 66 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is high (~1,680 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low GI), but the glycemic load per serving is approximately 34–35 (high), requiring portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is generally halal-suitable when prepared without pork-derived ingredients.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Lion's Mane Mushroom Thick Broth Yee Mee exists in any approved primary nutritional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked egg noodles, mushroom cooked, and chicken broth. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for cooked egg noodles. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA component data; no exact approved-source entry exists for this dish. 2. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate for a standard restaurant bowl; actual restaurant portion at Flavours by Saute - Funan may vary. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used for estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used for estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used for estimation. 6. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for the noodle and broth components used — no partially hydrogenated oils are assumed in this dish preparation. 7. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database cooked egg noodle entries; no direct GI measurement for Yee Mee with Lion's Mane broth was found. 8. The dish is classified as Chinese-Singaporean; multilingual names are provided in English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements. 9. Halal status is conditionally true assuming no pork-derived broth or lard is used; restaurant-specific verification is recommended. 10. Lion's Mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus) is increasingly available in Singapore hawker and restaurant settings and is associated with potential neuroprotective and immunomodulatory benefits in peer-reviewed literature, though these are not established clinical claims.
